chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُدْرِكُ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ بَعْضَ صَلَاتِهِ وَ يُحْدِثُ الْإِمَامُ فَيُقَدِّمُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 56 – A man attains part of his Salāt with the Imam, and the Imam breaks his ablution, so he asks him to lead

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/276)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/276)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي الرَّجُلِ إِذَا أَدْرَكَ الْإِمَامَ وَ هُوَ رَاكِعٌ فَكَبَّرَ وَ هُوَ مُقِيمٌ صُلْبَهُ ثُمَّ رَكَعَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَرْفَعَ الْإِمَامُ رَأْسَهُ فَقَدْ أَدْرَكَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Numan, from Ibn Muskan, from Sulaymen Bin Khalid who said,

‘Abu Abdullahasws said: ‘Regarding the man when he comes across the Imam he has performed Rukū, so he should exclaim a Takbīr while he is straight of back, then go into Rukū before the Imam raises his head, so he would have achieved (the Rak’at)’.
en.sarwar: 6. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Nu‘man from ibn Muskan from Sulayman ibn Khalid who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If a man finds the Imam in Ruku‘ (bowing down on one’s knees), says Takbir (Allah is great beyond description) while his back is straight and performs Ruku‘ before the Imam raises his head, has joined for one Rak‘at.’”
